lesson.menu_desc: Make a clear request with a deadline and send a polite reminder when no one answers -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Chasing a Reply -- A Clear Request & a Polite Reminder
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 18 -- Chasing a Reply

listenings.text: Hi Percival, this is Mr. Santos from the supplier. I am very sorry. I just saw your two emails about the contract, and I realize I never replied. It was my mistake. The matter is still pending on my side, but it is now urgent for me too. Your request is completely clear, and I am sorry you had to send a reminder. I am working on it today, and you have my word: you will have my full response by tomorrow morning. Thank you for being patient, and again, I apologize for the silence. Please do not worry. We will not miss the deadline. Have a good day.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I see the supplier is still awaiting our final numbers, and the report is now overdue. Could you chase this for me, please? Send them a short, polite reminder. Keep it kind, but be clear. Remind them that we are still waiting for their answer, that the request is urgent, and ask them to reply by Friday at the latest. Do not sound angry. Just say we kindly ask for a prompt response. If they do not reply today, we will call them on Monday. Thank you so much, and let me know when it is done.

extra_audio.text: First, Percival sends a clear request to the supplier and asks for an answer by Friday. Then, Friday passes and no response arrives. After that, he writes a short, polite reminder. Later, he says he is still waiting and kindly asks for a prompt reply. Finally, the supplier apologizes and promises a full response by the next morning.
